zh.lifeSummary: 當William Heath於大約1550年出生在英國時，他的父親Edward Heath26歲，母親Alice21歲。他於1580年6月9日與Agnes Cheney在英國艾可沃爾坦 艾比結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1625年1月7日在英國英格蘭賀特福爾韋爾享年76歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭賀特福爾普頓哈姆聖 馬也斯 亞德。

nameMeanings.name: Heath
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived ‘(on the) heath’ (Middle English he(a)th, Old English hǣth) or else a habitational name from any of numerous places called with this word. The same word also denoted heather, the characteristic plant of heathland areas. This surname has also been established in Dublin, Ireland, since the late 16th century.

variants.name: Haith
meanings: English (Lincolnshire and East Yorkshire): variant of   Heath  .

variants.name: Heathman
meanings: English: topographic name denoting a dweller ‘on the heath’, from Middle English he(a)th + man(ne). Compare Heath and Heather .

variants.name: Hatton
meanings: English: habitational name from any of several places called Hatton (Cheshire, Derbyshire, Lincolnshire, Middlesex, Shropshire, Staffordshire, Warwickshire), Hatton Hall (Cheshire), or Cold and High Hatton (Shropshire). The placenames derive from Old English hǣth ‘heath, heather’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Compare Heath .
English: variant of Atton with prosthetic H-, a topographic name from Middle English atte toun ‘(dweller) at the settlement’ (Old English æt thǣm tūn(e)). Compare Towne .
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Giolla Chatáin, a patronymic from a personal name meaning ‘servant of (Saint) Catan’; see McHatton .

variants.name: Heather
meanings: English: topographic name denoting a ‘dweller on the heath’, an agent derivative of Middle English hethe; compare Heath . The name may also be habitational name from Heather (Leicestershire), from Old English hǣthor ‘heathland’ (hǣth ‘heath, heather’ + the noun suffix -or) or hǣddre ‘heather’. This surname is widespread in southern England, and also established in Ireland.

variants.name: Health
meanings: Altered form of English and Scottish   Heath  .
English and Scottish: perhaps a nickname from the vocabulary word health. — Note: The population figure published by the US Census Bureau for this surname in the year 2000 is 105.

variants.name: Hoad
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived on a heath, from Middle English hoth ‘heath’ (Old English hāth, a byform of Old English hǣth), or a habitational name from a place so named, such as Hoath in Kent. This form was restricted in the Middle Ages to southeastern England, and the surname is still largely confined to Kent, Surrey, and Sussex. Compare Heath .

variants.name: Heid
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Dieter, Eckhart, Gerd, Gotthilf, Heinz, Klaus, Konrad, Kurt, Manfred, Ralf, Uli.
German, Swiss German, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): topographic name from Middle High German heide, German Heide ‘heath, moor, open, uncultivated land’. Compare Heath .
German: from the ancient Germanic personal name Haido, a short form of any of various compound names beginning with haid ‘heath’ (i.e. wasteland, often with heather).

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When William Heath was born about 1550, in England, his father, Edward Heath, was 26 and his mother, Alice, was 21. He married Agnes Cheney on 9 June 1580, in Waltham Abbey, Essex, England. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 7 January 1625, in Ware, Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 76, and was buried in St Mary's Churchyard, Puttenham, Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom.

ja.lifeSummary: William Heathが約1550年にイギリスで生まれた時，父Edward Heathは26歳，母Aliceは21歳であった。1580年6月9日にイギリスエセックスワルサム アビーでAgnes Cheneyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1625年1月7日にイギリスイギリスハートフォードシュアイアワーで76歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスハートフォードシュアイアプウッテンハムスト マリ'ス チャーチヤルドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: William Heath(이)가 대략 1550년 영국에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Edward Heath(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice(은)는 21세였다. 그는 1580년 6월 9일 영국 에세스 월섬 애브브이에서 Agnes Cheney(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1625년 1월 7일 영국 잉글랜드 허트퍼드실르 워에서 76세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 허트퍼드실르 풋텐함 스트 마이'스 처르c히아어드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William Heath nasceu aproximadamente 1550, em Inglaterra, seu pai, Edward Heath, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Alice, tinha 21 anos. Ele casou-se com Agnes Cheney em 9 de junho de 1580, em Waltham Abbey, Essex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 3 filhas. ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Heath родился около 1550 г. в местности Англия, его отцу по имени Edward Heath было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice, 21. Он женился на женщине по имени Agnes Cheney 9 июня 1580 г. в местности валтам эбби, эссекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 3 дочери. Он умер 7 января 1625 г. в местности вар, хиртфордшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 76 лет и был похоронен в местности ст мэри'с чурчярд, путтенам, хиртфордшир, Англия, Великобритания.
